steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The blank separates two independent clauses: the first ends with "supervised machine [learning]" and has a complete verb (works), the second begins with "Supervised machine learning" and has its own complete verb (involves).

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content:
Two independent clauses must be separated by a period, semicolon, or comma + coordinating conjunction — not a comma alone, and not nothing at all.

A) learning supervised — no punctuation between the two clauses; creates a run-on sentence. Wrong.
B) learning, supervised — a comma alone between two independent clauses is a comma splice. Wrong.
C) learning. Supervised — a period correctly ends the first clause and starts a new sentence. Correct.
D) learning, which supervised — turns the second clause into a relative clause fragment that distorts the meaning and leaves no main verb. Wrong.

C is correct.
